10-Key Typing (Numbers Only)

# 10-Key Typing (Numbers Only) test

Preview questions

## Summary of the 10 Key Typing (Numbers Only) test 

Our 10 Key Typing test evaluates candidates’ ability to accurately and efficiently enter numerical data using the ten keys on a full-sized keyboard’s number pad. It measures the **speed and accuracy** of a person’s data entry skills in keystrokes per hour (KPH).

### Covered skills

* Accurate and efficient numerical data entry under time constraints
 
* Ability to type randomly generated integer numbers between two and five digits long
 

### Use our 10 Key Speed test to hire

Data entry jobs, accountants, administrative assistants, secretaries, bookkeepers, data analysts, medical billing specialists, and any other role that requires fast and accurate numerical typing.

## About the 10 Key Typing test

Our 10 Key Typing test helps you evaluate a candidate’s data entry skills and ensure they have speed and accuracy. Hiring people who can enter numerical data quickly and efficiently is crucial for many business operations, from accounting and finance to sales and marketing.

Speedy and precise data entry enables **real-time decision-making, accurate financial reporting, and effective communication** with clients, customers, and other stakeholders. It also helps businesses identify trends and opportunities for strategic planning and decision-making.

With our Ten Key Speed test (Numbers only), you evaluate: 

* Numerical data speed and accuracy
 
* Proficiency using the numeric keypad (on the right side of most keyboards)
 
* Ability to handle high-volume data entry
 
* Efficiency in repetitive typing tasks
 

The prompts on our test provide a randomly generated sequence of integer numbers between two and five digits long. They don’t include mathematical operators or decimal points. As in most data entry software, job applicants must hit “enter” between entries. The results are displayed as a numeric typing speed in keystrokes per hour (KPH speed).

**View our** **preview question** **to see this test in action.**

This 10 Key Typing skills test supports job performance by minimizing errors and achieving compliance with regulatory requirements, such as tax reporting and auditing.

#### The test is made by a subject-matter expert

TestGorilla’s tests are created by subject matter experts. We assess potential subject-matter experts based on their knowledge, ability, and reputation.Before being published, each test is peer-reviewed by another expert, then calibrated using hundreds of test takers with relevant experience in the subject.

Our feedback mechanisms and unique algorithms allow our subject-matter experts to constantly improve their tests.

As with all the tests in our test library, the Typing Speed tests underwent assessment and review to ensure the tests meet the highest levels of accuracy before being published.

These tests will continue to be evaluated for constant improvement in line with our standardized revision process for all tests in our Test Library.

## Why are 10 key speed skills important to employers?

Good key typing speed helps employees perform well in data entry, accounting, or finance roles that rely on precise numerical input. As a result, you can experience several benefits when using a KPH test to hire employees for your business:

* **Increased productivity:** Employees can process data faster and complete more tasks in less time, which helps your business stay ahead of deadlines
 
* **Better accuracy:** Skilled typists enter data correctly, which helps you reduce costly errors in financial reports, inventory management, and important documents
 
* **Improved decision-making:** Fast and reliable data entry supports real-time data analysis, which enables you to respond quickly to market changes, customer needs, and emerging trends
 
* **Cost savings:** Efficient data entry reduces the need for extensive verification and correction processes, which saves time and resources
 
* **Precise reporting and compliance:** Strong 10 key speed skills help ensure that all entries meet compliance standards, supporting accurate tax reporting, auditing, and other regulatory requirements
 
* **Increased customer satisfaction:** These skills help employees manage invoices, process orders, and update records, which maintains high standards of customer service and satisfaction
 

Our skills tests can help you screen candidates quickly to discover whether they have the right numerical skills for the job and improve your return on investment.

For example, Ocean Outdoor UK used several role-specific tests and our Attention to Detail test to find new members for its finance team. Our talent assessments helped the company **hire faster and** **reduce unsuccessful hires by 44%**.

## What job roles can you hire with our 10 Key Typing test?

Our 10 Keystroke test is a versatile pre-employment typing test that helps you find candidates who excel in numerical data entry. 

Use our data entry 10 Key Typing test to evaluate employees and hire for these high volume positions:

* **Data entry specialists** must ensure rapid and accurate input of large volumes of numerical data
 
* **Accountants** need to maintain precise financial record-keeping and compliance with tax reporting requirements
 
* **Administrative assistants** support efficient data entry tasks, which improves office productivity
 
* **Secretaries** must display speed and accuracy in managing appointments, contact information, and other business data
 
* **Bookkeepers** guarantee accurate entry of financial transactions, aiding in reliable financial reporting
 
* **Data analysts** support in-depth data analysis and strategic planning
 
* **Medical billing specialists** need to master precise entry of billing codes and patient information for healthcare facilities
 

## Create a multi-measure assessment: 4 tests to pair with the 10 Key Typing (Numbers Only) test 

With TestGorilla, you can create a multi-measure assessment that goes beyond key data entry tests **to** **get a complete picture of your candidate’s skills and traits**. Combine this typing test for numbers with four other tests and find the right person for the job. 

Here’s a sample assessment we recommend for any of the jobs mentioned above:

* **Typing Speed (Lowercase Only) test**: Measure candidates’ ability to type quickly and accurately using lowercase letters, which complements their numerical touch typing skills
 
* **DISC Personality test**: Understand applicants’ behavioral traits and how they interact with others, which supports team collaboration and communication
 
* **Attention to Detail test**: Evaluate a candidate’s ability to notice and correct errors, which is crucial for maintaining accuracy in data entry tasks
 
* **Numerical Reasoning test**: Measure jobseekers’ ability to work with numbers and solve numerical problems, which is essential for roles that require strong analytical skills
 

*Note: We haven’t included any* *role-specific skills tests* *here because they depend on the position you’re hiring for. However, we highly recommend you add at least one in your five-test assessment to ensure your candidates possess the right skills for the job.*

## FAQs

An assessment is the total package of tests and custom questions that you put together to evaluate your candidates. Each individual test within an assessment is designed to test something specific, such as a job skill or language. An assessment can consist of up to 5 tests and 20 custom questions. You can have candidates respond to your custom questions in several ways, such as with a personalized video.

A video question is a specific type of custom question you can add to your assessment. Video questions let you create a question and have your candidates use their webcam to record a video response. This is an excellent way to see how a candidate would conduct themselves in a live interview, and is especially useful for sales and hospitality roles. Some good examples of things to ask for video questions would be "Why do you want to work for our company?" or "Try to sell me an item you have on your desk right now."

Yes! You can add your own logo and company color theme to your assessments. This is a great way to leave a positive and lasting brand impression on your candidates.

It depends! We offer five free tests, or unlimited access to our library of 400+ tests with the price based on your company size. Find more information on our pricing plans here, calculate the cost-benefit of using TestGorilla assessments, or speak to one of our sales team for your personalized demo and learn how we can help you revolutionize hiring today.

We recommend using our assessment software as a pre-screening tool at the beginning of your recruitment process. You can add a link to the assessment in your job post or directly invite candidates by email.

TestGorilla replaces traditional resume screening with a much more reliable and efficient process, designed to find the most skilled candidates earlier and faster.

Yes! Custom questions are great for testing candidates in your own unique way. We support the following question types: video, multiple-choice, coding, file upload, and essay. Besides adding your own custom questions, you can also create your own tests.

Besides video questions, you can also add the following types of custom questions: multiple-choice, coding, file upload, and essay. Multiple-choice lets your candidates choose from a list of answers that you provide, coding lets you create a coding problem for them to solve, file upload allows your candidates to upload a file that you request (such as a resume or portfolio), and essay allows an open-ended text response to your question. You can learn more about different custom question types here.

Our team is always here to help. After you sign up, we’ll reach out to guide you through the first steps of setting up your TestGorilla account. If you have any further questions, you can contact our support team via email, chat or call. We also offer detailed guides in our extensive help center.

Yes. You can add up to five tests to each assessment.

## Typing Speed (Lowercase Only)

Welcome to TestGorilla’s Typing Speed (Lowercase Only) test! Are you searching for candidates with exceptional typing skills? Our specialized test is designed to evaluate candidates’ typing speed in words per minute (WPM). By presenting a randomly generated sequence of lowercase words, we accurately measure their speed and accuracy. With this assessment, you can easily identify candidates who possess the typing proficiency required for your company. Gain valuable insights into their WPM score and make informed hiring decisions. Whether you’re hiring for administrative roles or positions that require strong typing abilities, our Typing Speed test is here to help you find the best talent.

2 min
